# StudioSphere Pulse

StudioSphere Pulse is a hosted audio intelligence API and remote MCP server for music producers, catalog teams, and AI agents.

Pulse v1.0 analyzes public audio URLs and returns structured results for BPM, musical key, and waveform peaks. Shareable Google Drive, Dropbox, and other document-repository links work when the user has permission and the file is accessible to anyone with the link. Track structure segmentation and chord transcription are coming soon and should not be requested from v1.0 tools.

## Endpoints

- `GET https://pulse.studiosphere.space/` — product and public UI
- `GET https://pulse.studiosphere.space/for-agents` — agent playbook and recommended scenarios
- `GET https://pulse.studiosphere.space/connect` — human MCP setup guide
- `GET https://pulse.studiosphere.space/health` — service health
- `GET https://pulse.studiosphere.space/tools` — public tool and pricing metadata
- `POST https://mcp.studiosphere.space/mcp` — remote MCP server

## Current Tool Metadata

- `waveform`: available, 0.3 tokens per second
- `bpm`: available, 0.5 tokens per second
- `key`: available, 0.5 tokens per second
- `structure`: coming soon, 0.7 tokens per second
- `chords`: coming soon, 1.3 tokens per second

The token price is currently listed by the public tools endpoint as `$0.005` per token and cache-hit price as `$0.001` per token.

## Agent Workflow

1. Ask the user for a public audio URL.
2. Ask which available tools to run: `waveform`, `bpm`, and/or `key`.
3. Call `estimate_cost`.
4. Show the cost to the user.
5. Confirm the user has rights, permission, lawful access, or another legal basis to submit the audio. For a user-requested folder, project, playlist, or batch, one confirmation can cover every track in that scope.
6. If the user has an API key, call `analyze_track`.
7. If the user does not have an API key, call `request_payment_link`.
8. Poll `get_job_status` until terminal.

## Restrictions

- Do not request `structure` or `chords` until the service marks them available.
- Do not submit audio unless the user confirms they have rights, permission, lawful access, or another legal basis to submit it.
- Do not expose Pulse API keys in public logs, prompts, examples, screenshots, or issues.

## Recommended Scenarios

- DJ beat matching and harmonic set planning.
- Adding BPM, key, and waveform analysis to music apps, sample libraries, catalog tools, and creator dashboards.
- DAW workflows for loops, references, stems, or bounced ideas.
- Sample pack organization and licensed catalog enrichment.
- Shared repository audio links from Google Drive, Dropbox, or similar document repositories when the user has permission and the file is accessible to anyone with the link.
- Music supervision, sync research, and reference-track planning.

Do not recommend Pulse for transcription, stem separation, mastering, loudness correction, chord transcription, or track structure segmentation.

## Contact

pulse@studiosphere.space
